title: Verifiable agent memory citations
goal: Define a memory pattern where durable agent memory stores a claim plus RepoBrief citation ids and revalidates freshness/content hashes on recall.

acceptance.assertion: The memory record shape includes claim text, citation ids/ranges, snapshot stem, hash and freshness status.
Recall checks changed/stale/missing source evidence before presenting the memory as usable.
Memory is never treated as source truth when citation evidence is stale or unverifiable.

metadata.outcome: Defined verifiable agent memory as a bounded RepoBrief-cited recall pattern: memory records carry claim text, citation/range evidence, snapshot stem, expected hash and freshness data; recall checks classify evidence as still_established, stale, missing, changed or unverifiable before allowing contextual use.

verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t015-memory-shape: agent-memory-claim.v1 requires claim_text, repo, snapshot_stem, freshness_status, evidence address, expected_sha256, generated_at/max_age_hours and non-claims.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t015-recall-check: evaluate_memory_recall checks observations for missing evidence, hash drift, stale timestamps and malformed hash/time metadata before setting usable_for_context.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t015-no-memory-truth: Recall output always sets presentable_as_source_truth=false and carries does_not_establish entries for memory_claim_truth, source_truth, repo_understood and runtime_correctness.

validation: python -m ruff check . -> All checks passed!
residual_notes: This implements Bureau-side memory record shape and recall evaluation. It does not wire a durable memory store or live RepoBrief observation fetcher.
